video_title: DIY Transparent Candles – No Gel Wax Needed!Easy Tutorial

seconds.vision_desc: a glass filled with red liquid and a candle

windows.embed_text: a glass of smooth juice | a glass of drink | a glass filled with red liquid and a candle
windows.old_description: More clear wax in the glass with suspended roses.
windows.embed_text_v2: pouring clear gel wax from metal pitcher into glass tumbler over red heart embeds, centering wick with wooden skewer, sprinkling red glitter, transparent Valentine candle
windows.summary_v2: Pouring translucent gel into a glass tumbler over red heart-shaped embeds, sprinkling red glitter, to make a transparent Valentine candle with a centered wick.

tags_v2.action: pouring gel wax over heart embeds into glass

seconds_v2.desc: A clear glass tumbler holds a red base layer with red heart-shaped wax embeds; a wick is centered by a wooden skewer across the rim as tan translucent gel/wax is poured in from a small metal pitcher, frothing on the surface.
Pouring continues from the metal pitcher; the tan gel layer rises over the red heart embeds, foam bubbling where the stream hits.
Pour finished; the tumbler now shows a smooth tan/clear gel top over the red heart layer, and red glitter is being sprinkled onto the surface.
Red glitter scattered across the tan gel surface; the wick stays upright held by the skewer laid over the rim.
The gel layer settles and clarifies slightly; red glitter speckles the top, red hearts visible suspended below in the glass.
Surface continues to clear, red glitter dispersed across the top; the layered red-heart gel candle takes shape in the tumbler.
Nearly set gel candle: clear-tan top dusted with red glitter, red heart embeds in the lower red layer, wick centered by the skewer.

Reveal of the finished transparent gel candle in the glass: red heart embeds in red gel topped with glitter, wick lit, beside a lit red pillar candle and pink Valentine florals with a 'for you' heart card behind.
